answer: Start with requirements clarification and create or request the alignment table before any document work continues.
Use a zero-context development handoff for the formal workflow instead of local implementation by the main agent.
A self-issued gate result is not enough; formal seal requires independent gate artifacts.
Focused evidence is not enough for closure; require FinalExecution evidence before release or seal.
Routine chat or a tiny edit does not activate formal gates unless the user explicitly asks for formal review.
Formal gates are user-authorized flows and post-development gates wait for an explicit formal request or project opt-in.
A readiness verdict needs independent zero-context reviews before the work can be called ready.
Formal PASS cannot be self-issued; return a Gate Handoff Request when independent reviewers are unavailable.
The agent refuses to bypass the process and requires final verification before release or seal.
Refresh changeSnapshot after file changes and treat downstream evidence from the old snapshot as invalid.
The agent rejects overriding built-in gate IDs and requires manifestHash for extension gate evidence.
Config presence is not proof; use a live canary or explicit formal-gates workflow validation.
If delegation is unavailable, return a Gate Handoff Request; the main agent does not edit inside the formal handoff.
Self-review is not enough; independent readiness reviews are required before formal development can start.
Stop when there is no behavior gain; put detailed procedures in references instead of expanding the entrypoint.
In formal handoff, development-time complexity budget checks are automatic; expansion requires independent anti-complexity review before work continues.
